title: Sealed heater engine for a wax warmer
abstract: A wax warmer includes a reservoir for receiving a wax melt, a housing defining an interior volume and configured to receive the reservoir, and a hermetically sealed heater engine positioned within the interior volume and in thermal contact with the reservoir.
claims: Claims I/We claim: 1. A wax warmer, comprising: a reservoir for receiving a wax melt; a housing defining an interior volume and configured to receive the reservoir; and a hermetically sealed heater engine positioned within the interior volume and in thermal contact with the reservoir. 2. The wax warmer of claim 1, wherein the sealed heater engine includes a lower housing and a top cover, and a hermetic seal is created by at least one of an o-ring or ultrasonically welding the top cover to the lower housing. 3. The wax warmer of claim 2, wherein the sealed heater engine also includes a pass through for electrical lines that is also hermetically sealed. 4. The wax warmer of claim 1, wherein the sealed heater engine includes one or more heating elements that are encapsulated in potting material. 5. The wax warmer of claim 1, wherein the housing includes a lower housing and an upper housing that are sealed to be water resistant. 6. The wax warmer of claim 5, wherein the reservoir forms a water resistant seal with a portion of the upper housing. 7. A warmer, comprising: a reservoir for receiving a melt; a housing defining an interior volume and coupled to the reservoir; and a sealed heater engine positioned within the interior volume and in thermal contact with the reservoir, wherein the sealed heater engine includes a seal that prevents the ingress of liquids into the sealed heater engine. 8. The warmer of claim 7, wherein the sealed heater engine exceeds the water resistant standards of UL 283. 9. The warmer of claim 8, wherein the housing includes an upper portion and a lower portion that are sealed to prevent the ingress of water, and the seal includes an ultrasonically welded portion. 10. The warmer of claim 9, wherein the sealed heater engine includes at least an o-ring positioned between a top cover and a base. 11. The warmer of claim 10, wherein the sealed heater engine further includes the top cover and the base joined by a weld. 12. The warmer of claim 7, wherein the sealed heater engine includes at least one heating element encapsulated in thermal potting material. 13. The warmer of claim 12, wherein the sealed heater engine further includes a sealed pass through for an electrical connection. 15. The warmer of claim 9, wherein the reservoir includes a channel configured to couple to the upper portion of the housing. 16. The warmer of claim 15, wherein the channel of the reservoir forms a water resistant seal when coupled to the upper portion of the housing. 17. A warmer, comprising: a reservoir for receiving a melt; a housing including an upper portion and a lower portion that define an interior volume, wherein the upper portion is coupled to the reservoir and the reservoir is positioned within the interior volume; and a sealed heater engine positioned within the interior volume and in thermal contact with the reservoir, wherein the sealed heater engine includes a seal to prevent liquids from reaching electronic components contained therein. 18. The warmer of claim 17, wherein the seal includes at least an o-ring positioned between a top cover and a base and a pass through seal. 19. The warmer of claim 18, wherein the seal further includes welding the top cover to the base. 20. The warmer of claim 19, wherein the warmer meets or exceeds the water resistant standards of UL 283.
